rows.concept: Go long/short when a fast oscillator (RSI) and a trend/momentum indicator (CCI) simultaneously confirm the same directional bias at bar close.
rows.eligibility_reason: Entry fully deterministic (RSI8>50 & CCI14>0 buy / RSI8<50 & CCI14<0 sell on candle close); exit named (reverse signal / trailing stop / TP) but stop and target distances unspecified.
rows.notes: Pairs EUR/USD, USD/CHF, EUR/JPY, GBP/USD (touches JPY cross). Best during European/London session (arubin, p17). Variants (all same core, one row): RSI 55/45 no-trade band instead of 50 (DirkH143 p4-5); RSI period 14 vs 8; CCI threshold +/-100 instead of 0 and CCI 89/21 or 170/34 (ClearTrade p16); ADX(14)>20 chop filter (arubin p17); 200-EMA trend filter (Zuke p5); H4/M30/M5 MTF confirmation (samer960 p19). Forum anecdote only ("400 pips in two days"), no backtest; author trades manually and declines EA. Weak edge fit: RSI and CCI are redundant momentum measures (arthurb p27: "they tell the same story") -> effectively a single-factor momentum crossover, low orthogonality.
